info.title: Kickflip
info.description: Kick those GIT changes and flip them over to that remote server

commands.name: deploy
commands.description: Kick your git changes and flip them over to that remote server!

options.name: --mode
options.description: The find mode to use when trying to determine the starting point to compare changes too HEAD with. [default: Tags]

options.name: --deployment-path
options.description: Deployment path on the remote server to deploy to. The deployment path is relative to the root of the user account on the remote server. [default: /]

options.name: --username
options.description: Authentication username for the remote server

options.name: --password
options.description: Authentication password for the remote server

options.name: --dry
options.description: Does a dry run of the deployment to see what would be done. No changes will be made to the remote server. [default: False]

arguments.name: path
arguments.description: Path to the folder containing the git repository for Git based deployment modes or the folder to use in folder deployment. The path is an absolute path. [default: <path>]

commands.name: github
commands.description: Integration with github to allow kickflip to work in your existing Github workflow.

commands.name: github pull-request
commands.description: Adds a comment to the pull request with the changes that will be deployed to the remote server.

options.name: --mode
options.description: The find mode to use when trying to determine the starting point to compare changes too HEAD with. [default: Tags]

options.name: --deployment-path
options.description: Deployment path on the remote server to deploy to. The deployment path is relative to the root of the user account on the remote server. [default: /]

options.name: --repo
options.description: The repository where the PR resides. Should be in the format of <owner>/<repository>.

options.name: --ref
options.description: The fully-formed ref of the branch or tag that triggered the workflow run. The ref given is fully-formed for pull requests it is refs/pull/<pr_number>/merge. See the GITHUB_REF variable in the GitHub Actions documentation. https://docs.github.com/en/actions/learn-github-actions/environment-variables

options.name: --token
options.description: The Github token or Personal access token to use for authentication. See the GITHUB_TOKEN variable in the GitHub Actions documentation. https://docs.github.com/en/actions/security-guides/automatic-token-authentication

arguments.name: local-path
arguments.description: Local path to the folder containing the git repository. The local path is an absolute path. [default: <path>]
